# services.ntopng

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.ntopng`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.ntopng.configText` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Overridable configuration file contents to use for ntopng. By default, use the contents automatically generated by NixOS. |
| `services.ntopng.enable` | `boolean` | Enable ntopng, a high-speed web-based traffic analysis and flow collection tool. With the default configuration, ntopng monitors all network interfaces and displays its findings at http://localhost:${toString config.services.ntopng.http-port}. Default username and password is admin/admin. See the {manpage}`ntopng(8)` manual page and http://www.ntop.org/products/ntop/ for more info. Note that enabling ntopng will also enable redis (key-value database server) for persistent data storage. |
| `services.ntopng.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Configuration lines that will be appended to the generated ntopng configuration file. Note that this mechanism does not work when the manual {option}`configText` option is used. |
| `services.ntopng.httpPort` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Sets the HTTP port of the embedded web server. |
| `services.ntopng.interfaces` | `list of string` | List of interfaces to monitor. Use "any" to monitor all interfaces. |
| `services.ntopng.redis.address` | `string` | Redis address - may be a Unix socket or a network host and port. |
| `services.ntopng.redis.createInstance` | `null or string` | Local Redis instance name. Set to `null` to disable local Redis instance. Defaults to `""` for `system.stateVersion` older than 22.05. |
